reasoning: As of early November 2025, the price of the COMEX Gold Continuous Contract is hovering around $3,900 to $4,000 per ounce, having recently reached all-time highs of over $4,000 (and even near $4,400 earlier). For the contract to close below $3,200 at the end of 2025, gold would have to crash by approximately 20% in less than two months. While commodities can be volatile, a 20% drop in such a short window is an extreme tail-risk event for gold. Consequently, it is highly likely (95%+) that the price will remain above the $3,200 threshold on December 31, 2025.
